name: Red Wine-And-Garlic Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 1 Beef round or rump roast (about 4 lb)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil over medium heat on the stove top in a heavy pot with a lid or Dutch oven.
2. Add the roast and surround it with carrots, celery, and garlic.
3. If cooking on the stove top, cover the pot tightly and reduce heat to low. Use a burner heat diffuser if available.
4. If using the oven method, cover tightly and place on the middle rack of the oven. Cook for 30 minutes.
5. Remove the cover, add the red wine, salt, pepper, bay leaves, and thyme. Replace the cover and continue to cook for 2½-to-3 hours.
6. Transfer the roast from the pot to a baking dish, cover, and keep warm in the turned-off or slow oven for up to 15 minutes.
7. Place the pot on top of the stove over medium heat and add the broth and butter. Cook, stirring vigorously until the butter is incorporated.
8. Remove the roast from the oven, slice into serving pieces, and arrange on a serving platter.
9. Strain the pan juices over the roast, pressing the vegetables against the sieve or strainer to extract all their juices. Serve immediately and pass any extra sauce in a sauceboat.
